reasoning: With no search tools available, the strongest signal is the market estimate from 2025-10-16, which priced the probability of inflation falling below 2.5% in 2025 under a Trump presidency at 13%. Trump's stated policies (e.g., universal baseline tariffs, higher tariffs on China, mass deportations, and extension of tax cuts) are widely considered highly inflationary by macroeconomists. Combined with already sticky services inflation from 2024, it makes it highly unlikely that YoY inflation would decline to <2.5% by late 2025. Tracking right in line with the recent market value, I place the probability at 13%.
